name: generate_env
description: Generates environment variable configuration files (e.g., .env) based on provided key-value pairs or from detected project needs (like database credentials after scaffolding). Use after scaffolding a new project or when new services requiring environment variables are added. This tool helps manage sensitive information and configurations required by the application at runtime.

project_path.description: The root directory path of the project where the .env file will be created or updated.

variables.description: An object where keys are environment variable names and values are their corresponding string values. Example: {"DATABASE_URL": "your_db_url_here", "API_KEY": "your_api_key_here"}. Provide this if you have specific key-value pairs to set.

auto_detect_services.description: An array of service names (e.g., ["supabase", "nextauth", "stripe"]) for which the tool should attempt to auto-generate common environment variables. The tool will look for typical variables associated with these services.

file_name.description: The name of the environment file to create or update. Defaults to '.env'.

hints.description: This tool is idempotent; running it multiple times with the same inputs will result in the same .env file state. It updates existing variables and adds new ones if specified.
